name: ormar (Async Python ORM for FastAPI)

auth.notes: ORM layer; auth handled by DB connection string

performance.notes: Async IO; performance similar to Databases library with Pydantic overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Async ORM combining SQLAlchemy core + Databases + Pydantic v1. Single model = Pydantic schema + ORM model. import ormar; import databases; import sqlalchemy. database = databases.Database('sqlite:///test.db'). metadata = sqlalchemy.MetaData(). class BaseMeta(ormar.ModelMeta): metadata=metadata; database=database. class Book(ormar.Model): class Meta(BaseMeta): tablename='books'. id: int = ormar.Integer(primary_key=True). title: str = ormar.String(max_length=200). await Book.objects.create(title='Dune'). books = await Book.objects.all(). book = await Book.objects.get(id=1). await book.update(title='New'). await book.delete(). FastAPI integration: natural since same Pydantic base. Development slowed (2023+). SQLModel (by FastAPI author) is an alternative.

gotchas: Pydantic v1 only — not compatible with Pydantic v2 as of 0.20
Migrations via Alembic are manual — no ormar-specific migration tool
Relationship handling more limited than SQLAlchemy ORM
SQLModel (tiangolo) is better maintained for FastAPI + Pydantic v2
best_when: FastAPI projects wanting single Pydantic+ORM model definition
Async Python apps where you want simple ORM without full SQLAlchemy
Projects already on Pydantic v1 ecosystem
avoid_when: New projects using Pydantic v2 (use SQLModel or SQLAlchemy 2.0)
Complex relational queries and joins (use SQLAlchemy ORM)
Projects needing active maintenance and community support
summary: ormar is an async Python ORM unifying SQLAlchemy core + Databases + Pydantic. class Book(ormar.Model): id = ormar.Integer(primary_key=True); title = ormar.String(max_length=200). await Book.objects.create(title='x'); await Book.objects.all(). Pydantic v1 only. MIT. Development slowed — prefer SQLModel for new FastAPI+Pydantic v2 projects. Good for existing Pydantic v1 async apps.
